subject: Ecology
title: Wetland Loss Worldwide
description: A micro-lesson that makes Wetland Loss Worldwide usable.

hook.text: The world has lost over 85% of its wetlands since 1700—faster than any other ecosystem.

buildup.text: Drainage for agriculture, urban expansion, and water diversion are the main drivers.

discovery.text: Wetlands are still disappearing three times faster than forests, yet they get far less attention.

twist.text: Many people view wetlands as wastelands—swamps to be drained, not ecosystems to be protected.

climax.text: The Ramsar Convention protects 2,400 wetland sites, but most of the world's wetlands remain unprotected.

punchline.text: We destroyed most wetlands before we understood them.

quiz.question: How much of the world's wetlands have been lost since 1700?
